meta.name: QIDS-SR16 Quick Inventory of Depressive Symptomatology
meta.fullName: Quick Inventory of Depressive Symptomatology - Self-Report 16-Item
meta.description: The QIDS-SR16 is a 16-item self-report scale that provides a detailed assessment of the nine DSM-defined symptom domains of depression: sad mood, concentration, self-criticism, suicidal ideation, interest, energy, sleep, appetite, and psychomotor changes.
meta.author: Rush AJ, et al. (2003)
meta.reference: Rush AJ, et al. The 16-Item Quick Inventory of Depressive Symptomatology (QIDS), clinician rating (QIDS-C), and self-report (QIDS-SR): a psychometric evaluation in patients with chronic major depression. Biol Psychiatry. 2003;54(5):573-583.
meta.instruction: Please select the response for each item that best describes you for the past seven days.

questions.text: Falling asleep: How long does it take you to fall asleep?
Sleep during the night: Do you wake up during the night (not counting trips to the bathroom)?
Waking up too early: Do you wake up before your planned wake-up time?
Sleeping too much: How much do you sleep each day (including naps)?
Feeling sad: How often and how intensely do you feel sad?
Decreased appetite: Has your appetite decreased?
Increased appetite: Has your appetite increased?
Weight decrease: Have you lost weight recently (not from dieting)?
Weight increase: Have you gained weight recently?
Concentration/Decision making: How is your ability to concentrate or make decisions?
Self-view: How do you view yourself?
Thoughts of death or suicide: Do you have thoughts of death or suicide?
General interest: What is your level of interest in activities or people?
Energy level: What is your energy level?
Psychomotor slowing: Do you feel slowed down in movements or speech?
Psychomotor agitation: Do you feel restless or need to move around?

ranges.label: No Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests no significant depressive symptoms. Your emotional functioning appears healthy, and you are not reporting notable changes in mood, energy, sleep, appetite, or concentration.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy lifestyle habits that support your mood
Regular exercise, social engagement, and adequate sleep are protective factors

ranges.label: Mild Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates mild depressive symptoms. You may be experiencing some changes in mood, energy, sleep patterns, or appetite. While not severe, these symptoms are worth monitoring as they may affect your daily comfort and functioning.
suggestions: Monitor your mood and symptoms over the coming weeks
Maintain regular physical activity and social connections
Practice stress management techniques such as mindfulness or relaxation exercises
Consult a healthcare provider if symptoms persist or worsen

ranges.label: Moderate Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate depressive symptoms that are likely affecting your daily functioning in areas such as work, relationships, or self-care. You may be experiencing persistent low mood, disrupted sleep, changes in appetite or weight, difficulty concentrating, or reduced interest in activities.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional for evaluation
Evidence-based treatments such as CBT and interpersonal therapy are effective at this level
Maintain a structured daily routine to provide stability
Stay connected with supportive people in your life
Avoid using alcohol or drugs to cope with low mood

ranges.label: Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe depressive symptoms that are significantly impairing your daily functioning and quality of life. You may be experiencing profound changes in sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, and feelings of hopelessness or worthlessness.
suggestions: Seek professional help from a mental health provider as soon as possible
If you have thoughts of self-harm or suicide, please call a crisis hotline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
A combination of psychotherapy and medication is often most effective for severe depression
Inform a trusted person about how you are feeling

ranges.label: Very Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates very severe depressive symptoms that require urgent professional attention. At this level, depression is likely causing profound impairment across all areas of your life. Effective treatments are available and seeking help immediately is critical.
suggestions: Seek immediate consultation with a psychiatrist or mental health professional
If you are in crisis or having thoughts of suicide, call 988 or go to your nearest emergency room
Do not stay alone; ensure someone you trust knows about your situation
Intensive treatment, potentially including hospitalization, may be appropriate
